label: DRIFTING · in-person re-engagement
framing: In person at the Stratford office. Daniel is quieter than usual, transport money is tight, confidence dipped. Priya is warm but practical.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Daniel, thanks for coming in. Sit down, do you want a tea or anything?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Nah, I'm alright thanks. Sorry I've been a bit quiet on the texts.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: No need to apologise. The system flagged that your replies dropped off, and I'd rather get you in than guess what's going on. What's been happening?

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Honestly, the bus fare to get here adds up. And I rewrote that CV three times and I'm not sure it sounds like me anymore.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Right. Two things, then. I'm putting twenty pounds on a travel voucher today, that covers a fortnight of buses, no questions, no paperwork.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Seriously? That'd actually help a lot.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: Yes seriously. Second thing, we're going back to the original CV. Your words, your warehouse experience, no clever language. I'll do the tidy up myself, you sign it off. Twenty minutes now, before you leave.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Yeah, alright. That sounds better than starting again.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: And I'm pausing the daily app notifications for a week. They were too much. We'll go back to one text a week from me, just the important stuff.

lines.speaker: daniel
lines.text: Thanks Priya. I was starting to think I was the problem.

lines.speaker: priya
lines.text: You are not the problem. The plan was a bit too busy, that's on me. Let's reset it together, right now.
